text: Abstract The relationship between the African Independent Churches Association (AICA) and the Umphumulo Lutheran Theological College (ULTC) in South Africa is analysed within the historical and theological framework. Focus is made on the fact that the AICs strive for leadership training in order to protect the doctrine whilst integrating indigenous religious experience with academic theological studies. The study describes the process of how the AICA collaborated with mainline denomination through going for studies at the ULTC after realising the complications in managing the AICA Theological College. The study reflects on the AICA students’ experiences as they underwent studies at the ULTC and on the challenges they encountered, as well as on the challenges related to the Lutheran World Federation (LWF)’s role towards the development of the AICs. Finally, the study strongly argues for ecumenical cooperation with a view to train a competent pastoral leadership in order to meet the socio-religious needs of the South African society.
